reason: The base case of the CDP hierarchy (line 1584) reads Tr_{B_1}(W^{(1)}) = 1 (scalar), but line 1587 claims that for N=1 this reduces to Tr_{B_1}(W) = I_{A_1}. These are inconsistent: for d_{A_1} > 1, Tr_{B_1}(W^{(1)}) is a d_{A_1} x d_{A_1} matrix, not a scalar. The correct formulation should either (a) define W^{(0)} = 1 and write the last recursive step as Tr_{B_1}(W^{(1)}) = I_{A_1} tensor W^{(0)} = I_{A_1}, or (b) write the base case directly as Tr_{B_1}(W^{(1)}) = I_{A_1}. As currently written, equating a matrix to the scalar 1 is a type error that propagates through all constraint counts.
